title: GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM - 211info
text: OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES GENERAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers short-term financial assistance for individuals applying for Social Security benefits. Support is also available with applications and appeals. Participants are required to repay part of the award received. This is not an emergency service, and applications may take 3–6 months to process. 500 Summer Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Must meet all of the following criteria: -Ages 18-64 with no minor children in the household -Homeless or at imminent risk of homelessness -Have a condition that meets Social Security Disability Insurance (SSDI) or Supplemental Security Income (SSI) qualifying criteria -Are not currently receiving SSDI or SSI -Have already applied or plan to apply for SSDI or SSI and if the latter are filing an appeal if the awards have been denied -Be receiving Presumptive Medicaid assistance Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (866) 535-8431 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (866) 535-8431 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call or email for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: State of Oregon Services: Undesignated Temporary Financial Assistance * Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Undesignated Temporary Financial Assistance * Homelessness Issues Undesignated Temporary Financial Assistance * Social Security Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - INTELLECTUAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON DISABILITIES COMMISSION INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - CHILDREN'S INTENSIVE IN-HOME SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON DEAF AND HARD OF HEARING SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - OREGON HOME CARE COMMISSION INFORMATION SERVICES - BRAIN INJURY PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON STATE DEPARTMENT OF HUMAN SERVICES SENIORS AND PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES
